title: Support for Unicode in the SYB Connector
breadcrumbs: Support for Unicode in the SYB Connector
text: Support for Unicode in the SYB Connector The Uniface SYB connector can support Unicode. By default, Unicode support is disabled. When Unicode is enabled, Unicode characters can be used in: Fields with wide character packing codes (such as W10, SW* ) SQL Workbench and the sql ProcScript statement Sybase stored procedures Uniface Service Stored Procedures (SSP) Important: Unicode is not supported in table names, column names, or user names and passwords. You can enable and configure Unicode support by setting connector options in the application assignment file. The following connector options and settings determine where Unicode characters can be used: unicode —enables Unicode for fields with wide-character packing codes. By default, this is limited to characters in the Basic Multilingual Plane (BMP). smp —extends Unicode support to include the Supplementary Multilingual Plane (SMP) in W packing codes. charset utf8 —enables Unicode characters to be used in SQL Workbench and the sql ProcScript statement. keyword —distinguishes between data and column names, so that Unicode can be used in the SQL Workbench and the sql ProcScript statement. $def_charset =UTF8 —ensures that Unicode is also supported in Uniface Service Stored Procedures (SSP) and Sybase stored procedures. Related Links Uniface Support for Unicode Uniface supports Unicode in the user interface, database storage and retrieval, sorting, case conversion, and data exchange. Uniface uses Unicode UTF-8 encoding for as its internal character set. $DEF_CHARSET Specify the default character set used for storing data in String fields with C packing code.
